pattern: Only the distance and parity matter: the count is the binomial coefficient choosing the right-step total, reducing the walk to one combination modulo a prime.
hints: Let d be the distance between start and end; the direction of the line does not change the count.
If r is the number of right steps and l the left, then r + l = k and r - l = d, which fixes r exactly when k and d share parity.
When (k + d) is even and r = (k + d) / 2 lies in [0, k], the answer is C(k, r) mod 1e9+7; otherwise it is 0.

edge_inputs.description: small reachable case with three paths
return to start with even steps
return to start needs even steps, odd fails
large k producing a big binomial under mod

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s = rng.randrange(1, 1001)
    e = rng.randrange(1, 1001)
    k = rng.randrange(1, size + 1)
    return [s, e, k]
stress.note: covers both parity-matching and mismatching draws
